edge_reviews.notes: Medical workflows combine text, images, and clinical data; multimodal foundation use is defensible synthesis.
CNNs are standard image-recognition models and align with medical imaging; common strength is defensible.
Clinical decision support requires calibrated risk/uncertainty; core strength is defensible.
Medical data use requires privacy/governance controls; common strength is defensible.
Linear/GLM models are common clinical prediction baselines/components; common strength is defensible.
Gaussian processes are plausible uncertainty models but not directly supported for this application in the packet.
Medical imaging directly uses visual perception/image-analysis methods; optional strength is defensible.
Formal verification is relevant to high-assurance ML but packet evidence does not establish medical-AI dependency strength.
Causal methods are relevant clinically, but the packet lacks direct evidence for causal representation learning as a dependency.
Semi/weak supervision is plausible where labels are scarce or noisy in clinical data; optional strength is defensible synthesis.
ResNets/DenseNets are image-recognition architectures relevant to medical imaging; optional strength is defensible.
Fairness and subgroup analysis are relevant to clinical decision support; optional strength is defensible.
